api.name: CameraPreviewPlugin
api.docs: The main interface for the CameraPreview plugin.

methods.name: start
methods.signature: (options: CameraPreviewOptions) => Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The configuration for the camera preview.

methods.returns: Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The configuration for the camera preview.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the preview dimensions.

methods.docs: Starts the camera preview.

methods.name: stop

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - Optional configuration for stopping the camera.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Optional configuration for stopping the camera.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the camera preview is stopped.

methods.docs: Stops the camera preview.

methods.name: capture
methods.signature: (options: CameraPreviewPictureOptions) => Promise<{ value: string; exif: ExifData; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The options for capturing the picture.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The options for capturing the picture.

tags.name: returns
tags.text:
Resolves with:
- `value`: base64 string, or file path if `storeToFile` is true
- `exif`: extracted EXIF metadata when available

methods.docs:
Captures a picture from the camera.

If `storeToFile` was set to `true` when starting the preview, the returned
`value` will be an absolute file path on the device instead of a base64 string. Use getBase64FromFilePath to get the base64 string from the file path.

methods.name: captureSample

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The options for capturing the sample.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The options for capturing the sample.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the sample image as a base64 encoded string.

methods.docs: Captures a single frame from the camera preview stream.

methods.name: getSupportedFlashModes

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with an array of supported flash modes.

methods.docs: Gets the flash modes supported by the active camera.

methods.name: setAspectRatio
methods.signature: (options: { aspectRatio: '4:3' | '16:9'; x?: number; y?: number; }) => Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs:
- The desired aspect ratio and optional position.
- aspectRatio: The desired aspect ratio ('4:3' or '16:9')
- x: Optional x coordinate for positioning. If not provided, view will be auto-centered horizontally.
- y: Optional y coordinate for positioning. If not provided, view will be auto-centered vertically.
parameters.type: { aspectRatio: '4:3' | '16:9'; x?: number | undefined; y?: number | undefined; }

methods.returns: Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text:
options - The desired aspect ratio and optional position.
- aspectRatio: The desired aspect ratio ('4:3' or '16:9')
- x: Optional x coordinate for positioning. If not provided, view will be auto-centered horizontally.
- y: Optional y coordinate for positioning. If not provided, view will be auto-centered vertically.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the actual preview dimensions and position.

methods.docs: Set the aspect ratio of the camera preview.

methods.name: getAspectRatio

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the current aspect ratio.

methods.docs: Gets the current aspect ratio of the camera preview.

methods.name: setGridMode

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The desired grid mode ('none', '3x3', or '4x4').

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the grid mode is set.

methods.docs: Sets the grid mode of the camera preview overlay.

methods.name: getGridMode

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the current grid mode.

methods.docs: Gets the current grid mode of the camera preview overlay.

methods.name: checkPermissions

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: Set `disableAudio` to `false` to also include microphone status (defaults to `true`).

tags.name: param
tags.text: options Set `disableAudio` to `false` to also include microphone status (defaults to `true`).

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise resolving to the current authorization states.

methods.docs: Checks the current camera (and optionally microphone) permission status without prompting the system dialog.

methods.name: requestPermissions

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - Configuration for the permission request behaviour.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - Configuration for the permission request behaviour.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise resolving to the final authorization states.

methods.docs:
Requests camera (and optional microphone) permissions. If permissions are already granted or denied,
the current status is returned without prompting. When `showSettingsAlert` is true and permissions are denied,
a platform specific alert guiding the user to the app settings will be presented.

methods.name: getHorizontalFov

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the horizontal field of view in degrees.

methods.docs:
Gets the horizontal field of view (FoV) for the active camera.
Note: This can be an estimate on some devices.

methods.name: getSupportedPictureSizes

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the list of supported sizes.

methods.docs: Gets the supported picture sizes for all cameras.

methods.name: setFlashMode

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the flash mode is set.

methods.docs: Sets the flash mode for the active camera.

methods.name: flip

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the camera is flipped.

methods.docs: Toggles between the front and rear cameras.

methods.name: setOpacity

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the opacity is set.

methods.docs: Sets the opacity of the camera preview.

methods.name: stopRecordVideo

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the path to the recorded video file.

methods.docs: Stops an ongoing video recording.

methods.name: startRecordVideo

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The options for video recording. Only iOS.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The options for video recording. Only iOS.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when video recording starts.

methods.docs: Starts recording a video.

methods.name: isRunning

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the running state.

methods.docs: Checks if the camera preview is currently running.

methods.name: getAvailableDevices

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the list of available camera devices.

methods.docs: Gets all available camera devices.

methods.name: getZoom
methods.signature: () => Promise<{ min: number; max: number; current: number; lens: LensInfo; }>
methods.returns: Promise<{ min: number; max: number; current: number; lens: LensInfo; }>

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the zoom state.

methods.docs: Gets the current zoom state, including min/max and current lens info.

methods.name: getZoomButtonValues
methods.docs:
Returns zoom button values for quick switching.
- iOS/Android: includes 0.5 if ultra-wide available; 1 and 2 if wide available; 3 if telephoto available
- Web: unsupported

methods.name: setZoom
methods.signature: (options: { level: number; ramp?: boolean; autoFocus?: boolean; }) => Promise<void>

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The desired zoom level. `ramp` is currently unused. `autoFocus` defaults to true.
parameters.type: { level: number; ramp?: boolean | undefined; autoFocus?: boolean | undefined; }

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The desired zoom level. `ramp` is currently unused. `autoFocus` defaults to true.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the zoom level is set.

methods.docs: Sets the zoom level of the camera.

methods.name: getFlashMode

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the current flash mode.

methods.docs: Gets the current flash mode.

methods.name: removeAllListeners
methods.docs: Removes all registered listeners.

methods.name: setDeviceId

parameters.name: options
parameters.docs: - The ID of the device to switch to.

tags.name: param
tags.text: options - The ID of the device to switch to.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the camera is switched.

methods.docs: Switches the active camera to the one with the specified `deviceId`.

tags.name: returns

methods.name: getPreviewSize
methods.signature: () => Promise<{ x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number; }>
methods.returns: Promise<{ x: number; y: number; width: number; height: number; }>
methods.docs: Gets the current preview size and position.

methods.name: setPreviewSize
methods.signature: (options: { x?: number; y?: number; width: number; height: number; }) => Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { x?: number | undefined; y?: number | undefined; width: number; height: number; }

methods.returns: Promise<{ width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }>

tags.name: param
tags.text: options The new position and dimensions.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with the actual preview dimensions and position.

methods.docs: Sets the preview size and position.

methods.name: setFocus

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves when the focus is set.

methods.docs:
Sets the camera focus to a specific point in the preview.

Note: The plugin does not attach any native tap-to-focus gesture handlers. Handle taps in
your HTML/JS (e.g., on the overlaying UI), then pass normalized coordinates here.

methods.name: addListener
methods.signature: (eventName: 'screenResize', listenerFunc: (data: { width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }) => void) => Promise<PluginListenerHandle>

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.docs: - The event name to listen for.

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - The function to call when the event is triggered.
parameters.type: (data: { width: number; height: number; x: number; y: number; }) => void

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name to listen for.

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - The function to call when the event is triggered.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with a handle to the listener.

methods.docs: Adds a listener for screen resize events.

methods.name: addListener

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.docs: - The event name to listen for.

parameters.name: listenerFunc
parameters.docs: - The function to call when the event is triggered.

tags.name: param
tags.text: eventName - The event name to listen for.

tags.name: param
tags.text: listenerFunc - The function to call when the event is triggered.

tags.name: returns
tags.text: A promise that resolves with a handle to the listener.

methods.docs: Adds a listener for orientation change events.

methods.name: deleteFile
methods.docs:
Deletes a file at the given absolute path on the device.
Use this to quickly clean up temporary images created with `storeToFile`.
On web, this is not supported and will throw.

methods.name: getSafeAreaInsets
methods.docs:
Gets the safe area insets for devices.
Returns the orientation-aware notch/camera cutout inset and the current orientation.
In portrait mode: returns top inset (notch at top).
In landscape mode: returns left inset (notch moved to side).
This specifically targets the cutout area (notch, punch hole, etc.) that all modern phones have.

Android: Values returned in dp (logical pixels).
iOS: Values returned in physical pixels, excluding status bar (only pure notch/cutout size).

methods.name: getOrientation
methods.docs: Gets the current device orientation in a cross-platform format.

methods.name: getExposureModes
methods.docs:
Returns the exposure modes supported by the active camera.
Modes can include: 'locked', 'auto', 'continuous', 'custom'.

methods.name: getExposureMode
methods.docs: Returns the current exposure mode.

methods.name: setExposureMode
methods.docs: Sets the exposure mode.

methods.name: getExposureCompensationRange
methods.signature: () => Promise<{ min: number; max: number; step: number; }>
methods.returns: Promise<{ min: number; max: number; step: number; }>
methods.docs: Returns the exposure compensation (EV bias) supported range.

methods.name: getExposureCompensation
methods.docs: Returns the current exposure compensation (EV bias).

methods.name: setExposureCompensation
methods.docs: Sets the exposure compensation (EV bias). Value will be clamped to range.

methods.name: getPluginVersion

tags.name: returns
tags.text: an Promise with version for this device

tags.name: throws
tags.text: An error if the something went wrong

methods.docs: Get the native Capacitor plugin version

interfaces.name: CameraPreviewOptions
interfaces.docs: Defines the configuration options for starting the camera preview.

properties.name: parent
properties.docs: The parent element to attach the video preview to.

properties.name: className
properties.docs: A CSS class name to add to the preview element.

properties.name: width
properties.docs: The width of the preview in pixels. Defaults to the screen width.

properties.name: height
properties.docs: The height of the preview in pixels. Defaults to the screen height.

properties.name: x
properties.docs: The horizontal origin of the preview, in pixels.

properties.name: y
properties.docs: The vertical origin of the preview, in pixels.

properties.name: aspectRatio
properties.docs:
The aspect ratio of the camera preview, '4:3' or '16:9' or 'fill'.
Cannot be set if width or height is provided, otherwise the call will be rejected.
Use setPreviewSize to adjust size after starting.

properties.name: aspectMode
properties.docs:
Controls how the camera preview fills the available space.
- 'contain': Fits the entire preview within the space, may show letterboxing (default).
- 'cover': Fills the entire space, may crop edges of the preview.

properties.name: gridMode
properties.docs: The grid overlay to display on the camera preview.

properties.name: includeSafeAreaInsets
properties.docs: Adjusts the y-position to account for safe areas (e.g., notches).

properties.name: toBack
properties.docs: If true, places the preview behind the webview.

properties.name: paddingBottom
properties.docs: Bottom padding for the preview, in pixels.

properties.name: rotateWhenOrientationChanged
properties.docs: Whether to rotate the preview when the device orientation changes.

properties.name: position
properties.docs: The camera to use.

properties.name: storeToFile
properties.docs:
If true, saves the captured image to a file and returns the file path.
If false, returns a base64 encoded string.

properties.name: disableExifHeaderStripping
properties.docs: If true, prevents the plugin from rotating the image based on EXIF data.

properties.name: disableAudio
properties.docs: If true, disables the audio stream, preventing audio permission requests.

properties.name: lockAndroidOrientation
properties.docs: If true, locks the device orientation while the camera is active.

properties.name: enableOpacity
properties.docs: If true, allows the camera preview's opacity to be changed.

properties.name: disableFocusIndicator
properties.docs: If true, disables the visual focus indicator when tapping to focus.

properties.name: deviceId
properties.docs: The `deviceId` of the camera to use. If provided, `position` is ignored.

properties.name: initialZoomLevel
properties.docs:
The initial zoom level when starting the camera preview.
If the requested zoom level is not available, the native plugin will reject.

properties.name: positioning
properties.docs: The vertical positioning of the camera preview.

properties.name: enableVideoMode
properties.docs: If true, enables video capture capabilities when the camera starts.

properties.name: force
properties.docs:
If true, forces the camera to start/restart even if it's already running or busy.
This will kill the current camera session and start a new one, ignoring all state checks.

properties.name: videoQuality

tags.text: On Android requires 'enableVideoMode' to be true
tags.name: note

tags.text: Will affect the entire preview stream for iOS
tags.name: note

properties.docs:
Sets the quality of video for recording.
Options: 'low', 'medium', 'high'

interfaces.name: ExifData
interfaces.docs: Represents EXIF data extracted from an image.

interfaces.name: CameraPreviewPictureOptions
interfaces.docs: Defines the options for capturing a picture.

properties.name: height
properties.docs:
The maximum height of the picture in pixels. The image will be resized to fit within this height while maintaining aspect ratio.
If not specified the captured image will match the preview's visible area.

properties.name: width
properties.docs:
The maximum width of the picture in pixels. The image will be resized to fit within this width while maintaining aspect ratio.
If not specified the captured image will match the preview's visible area.

properties.name: quality
properties.docs:
The quality of the captured image, from 0 to 100.
Does not apply to `png` format.

properties.name: format
properties.docs: The format of the captured image.

properties.name: saveToGallery
properties.docs: If true, the captured image will be saved to the user's gallery.

properties.name: withExifLocation
properties.docs:
If true, the plugin will attempt to add GPS location data to the image's EXIF metadata.
This may prompt the user for location permissions.

properties.name: embedTimestamp
properties.docs: If true, the plugin will embed a timestamp in the top-right corner of the image.

properties.name: embedLocation
properties.docs:
If true, the plugin will embed the current location in the top-right corner of the image.
Requires `withExifLocation` to be enabled.

properties.name: photoQualityPrioritization
properties.docs:
Sets the priority for photo quality vs. capture speed.
- "speed": Prioritizes faster capture times, may reduce image quality.
- "balanced": Aims for a balance between quality and speed.
- "quality": Prioritizes image quality, may reduce capture speed.
See https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avcapturephotosettings/photoqualityprioritization for details.

interfaces.name: CameraSampleOptions
interfaces.docs: Defines the options for capturing a sample frame from the camera preview.

properties.name: quality
properties.docs: The quality of the captured sample, from 0 to 100.

interfaces.name: SupportedPictureSizes
interfaces.docs: Represents the supported picture sizes for a camera facing a certain direction.

properties.name: facing
properties.docs: The camera direction ("front" or "rear").

properties.name: supportedPictureSizes
properties.docs: A list of supported picture sizes for this camera.

interfaces.name: PictureSize
interfaces.docs: Defines a standard picture size with width and height.

properties.name: width
properties.docs: The width of the picture in pixels.

properties.name: height
properties.docs: The height of the picture in pixels.

interfaces.name: CameraOpacityOptions
interfaces.docs: Defines the options for setting the camera preview's opacity.

properties.name: opacity
properties.docs: The opacity percentage, from 0.0 (fully transparent) to 1.0 (fully opaque).

interfaces.name: CameraDevice
interfaces.docs: Represents a physical camera on the device (e.g., the front-facing camera).

properties.name: deviceId
properties.docs: A unique identifier for the camera device.

properties.name: label
properties.docs: A human-readable name for the camera device.

properties.name: position
properties.docs: The physical position of the camera on the device.

properties.name: lenses
properties.docs: A list of all available lenses for this camera device.

properties.name: minZoom
properties.docs: The overall minimum zoom factor available across all lenses on this device.

properties.name: maxZoom
properties.docs: The overall maximum zoom factor available across all lenses on this device.

properties.name: isLogical
properties.docs: Identifies whether the device is a logical camera (composed of multiple physical lenses).

interfaces.name: CameraLens
interfaces.docs: Represents a single camera lens on a device. A {@link CameraDevice} can have multiple lenses.

properties.name: label
properties.docs: A human-readable name for the lens, e.g., "Ultra-Wide".

properties.name: deviceType
properties.docs: The type of the camera lens.

properties.name: focalLength
properties.docs: The focal length of the lens in millimeters.

properties.name: baseZoomRatio
properties.docs: The base zoom factor for this lens (e.g., 0.5 for ultra-wide, 1.0 for wide).

properties.name: minZoom
properties.docs: The minimum zoom factor supported by this specific lens.

properties.name: maxZoom
properties.docs: The maximum zoom factor supported by this specific lens.

interfaces.name: LensInfo
interfaces.docs: Represents the detailed information of the currently active lens.

properties.name: focalLength
properties.docs: The focal length of the active lens in millimeters.

properties.name: deviceType
properties.docs: The device type of the active lens.

properties.name: baseZoomRatio
properties.docs: The base zoom ratio of the active lens (e.g., 0.5x, 1.0x).

properties.name: digitalZoom
properties.docs: The current digital zoom factor applied on top of the base zoom.

interfaces.name: SafeAreaInsets
interfaces.docs:
Represents safe area insets for devices.
Android: Values are expressed in logical pixels (dp) to match JS layout units.
iOS: Values are expressed in physical pixels and exclude status bar.

properties.name: orientation
properties.docs: Current device orientation (1 = portrait, 2 = landscape, 0 = unknown).

properties.name: top
properties.docs:
Orientation-aware notch/camera cutout inset (excluding status bar).
In portrait mode: returns top inset (notch at top).
In landscape mode: returns left inset (notch at side).
Android: Value in dp, iOS: Value in pixels (status bar excluded).

typeAliases.name: CameraPreviewFlashMode
typeAliases.docs:
The available flash modes for the camera.
'torch' is a continuous light mode.

typeAliases.name: Pick
typeAliases.docs: From T, pick a set of properties whose keys are in the union K

typeAliases.name: DeviceOrientation
typeAliases.docs: Canonical device orientation values across platforms.

typeAliases.name: ExposureMode
typeAliases.docs: Reusable exposure mode type for cross-platform support.
